title: Software Engineer - Python (Advanced)
description:
**Software Engineer – Python (Advanced)**
**About The Role**
 What if your Python expertise could directly influence how AI systems reason about, write, and evaluate real\\-world code? We're looking for advanced Python engineers to work on challenging, high\\-impact problems — building and refining the software and data workflows that power next\\-generation AI applications.
   

  

 This is a fully remote, flexible contract role designed for engineers who thrive on complex problems, clean architecture, and sharp analytical thinking. If you've spent years writing production\\-grade Python and want work that actually stretches your skills, this is it.
   

  

* Organization: Alignerr
* Type: Hourly Contract
* Location: Remote
* Commitment: 10–40 hours/week


**What You'll Do**
* Design, implement, and optimize advanced Python code for production\\-level systems and AI\\-driven workflows
* Analyze and manipulate structured datasets to support engineering and modeling pipelines
* Write and optimize complex SQL queries to extract, transform, and validate data
* Review, debug, and improve existing codebases for performance, reliability, and long\\-term maintainability
* Translate ambiguous, high\\-level requirements into precise, well\\-reasoned technical solutions
* Apply consulting\\-style thinking to evaluate tradeoffs and recommend best\\-fit approaches
* Document assumptions, decisions, and implementation details with clarity and precision


**Who You Are**
**Must\\-Have**
* 4\\+ years of professional software engineering experience
* Advanced Python proficiency — clean architecture, performance optimization, debugging at depth
* Strong data analysis experience working with real\\-world, complex datasets
* Solid SQL skills, including complex queries, joins, and data validation workflows
* Prior consulting or client\\-facing experience, with a demonstrated ability to reason through open\\-ended technical problems


**Nice to Have**
* Experience working with large, messy, or unstructured datasets
* Familiarity with backend systems, REST APIs, or data pipelines
* Comfort reviewing and evaluating AI\\-generated code or analytical outputs
* Strong written communication with sharp attention to detail


**Why Join Us**
* Work on cutting\\-edge AI projects alongside leading research labs
* Fully remote and flexible — work when and where you do your best thinking
* Freelance autonomy with the structure of meaningful, task\\-based engineering work
* Make a direct, tangible impact on how AI understands and produces high\\-quality software
* Potential for ongoing work and contract extension as new projects launch

title: Software Engineer
description:
**Company Description** 




 Microfactory.tech specializes in providing custom manufacturing and engineering solutions to customers across various industries on a global scale. With an extensive, verified supplier network, Microfactory ensures high\\-quality production, from concept design to prototype and final manufacturing, all while maintaining competitive pricing. The company emphasizes end\\-to\\-end value\\-added sourcing services, enabling clients to focus on innovation while Microfactory handles product development, warehousing, and logistics. By leveraging cost\\-effective production hubs such as India, Microfactory helps clients significantly reduce costs and tariffs, especially for Western markets.
 



  





**Role Description** 




 This is a full\\-time remote role for a Software Engineer Intern. The intern will be responsible for writing, testing, and debugging code, contributing to projects focused on back\\-end web development, collaborating with team members to create efficient software solutions, and assisting the team in implementing and troubleshooting object\\-oriented programming tasks. The intern will also participate in team meetings, complete technical documentation, and learn essential software development practices. We are deveoping inhouse quoting instant tool. We are developing an order tracking platform along with SaaS development for supplier and customer partners.
 



  





 We're building
 **Microfactory OS** 
 — the operating system that runs the entire business: customer RFQs and quotes, order management, supplier programs, QC, dispatch, and full order trace visibility. This role owns the platform.
 



  





 You will be the founding (or early) software engineer building and scaling Microfactory OS. The platform is already live with real customers, suppliers, and PMs using it daily. You'll work directly with the founder (Mitul) on product, architecture, and execution — shipping features end\\-to\\-end, fixing real bugs that real users report, and building toward a long\\-term vision of an AI\\-assisted global manufacturing platform.
 



 This is not a maintenance role. The codebase is young, the roadmap is wide open, and your judgment on what to build and how will materially shape the company.
 



  




* Customer portal (RFQ submission, quote review, order tracking, document management, PM messaging)
* Supplier portal (program intake, QC submissions, dispatch confirmations)
* Admin/PM portal (order lifecycle management, supplier selection, quote generation, team and user management)
* AI\\-powered features: supplier selection and evaluation, demand planning, RFQ\\-to\\-quote configuration
* Order Trace Visibility Platform (in progress) — real\\-time visibility from supplier floor to US warehouse to customer dock
* Integrations with shipping, customs, accounting, and communication tools



 Tools:
 


* **Frontend:** 
 Next.js (App Router), React, TypeScript
* **Backend:** 
 Next.js API routes, Supabase (Postgres, Auth, Edge Functions, Storage, RLS)
* **AI:** 
 Anthropic API (Claude) for quote configuration, supplier evaluation, demand planning
* **Email/Comms:** 
 Resend SMTP via Supabase Auth
* **Infra:** 
 Vercel (hosting), GitHub (source control), Supabase Cloud
* **Future:** 
 Likely additions in workflow orchestration, observability, mobile



  





**Required** 



* 2\\+ years of professional software engineering experience shipping production web apps
* Strong proficiency with TypeScript, React, and Next.js (App Router preferred)
* Solid SQL skills and hands\\-on Postgres experience — comfortable writing migrations, designing schemas, debugging RLS policies, and reading EXPLAIN plans
* Experience with Supabase, Firebase, or a comparable BaaS — you understand auth flows, service\\-role vs anon key boundaries, and row\\-level security
* Comfort owning features end\\-to\\-end: schema → API → UI → deploy → debug in production
* Clear written communication — you can explain a tradeoff in three sentences



**Preferred** 



* Experience integrating LLM APIs (Anthropic, OpenAI) into production features
* Background in B2B SaaS, ERP, supply chain, manufacturing, or logistics software
* Familiarity with Vercel deployment workflows, GitHub Actions, and incident debugging
* Experience with multi\\-tenant architectures and role\\-based access control
* Comfort working in a small team where priorities shift weekly



**You'll thrive here if** 



* You like building zero\\-to\\-one and don't need a 30\\-page spec to start
* You're comfortable being the person who owns a bug from "user reports issue" to "fix is in production."
* You enjoy talking to non\\-technical operators (PMs, customers, suppliers) and translating their problems into software
* You believe small teams ship faster, and you want to be one of the early names on something that becomes a category\\-defining manufacturing platform

* 4\\+ years’ professional experience in software development.

* Strong grasp of Agile methodologies, DevOps principles, application security and version control systems (e.g., Azure DevOps).
* Proficiency in modern front\\-end frameworks (e.g., preference: React; Angular).

**Why Husky?**

* Cutting\\-Edge Projects: Work on innovative cloud solutions that are transforming our and our customer’s business, giving you the opportunity to be at the forefront of technological advancements.

 Husky TechnologiesTM offers a competitive compensation and benefits package and excellent opportunities for growth and advancement. title: Boat Engineer
description:
Amentum, a global leader in advanced engineering, technical, and professional services serving the US government, allies and critical infrastructure sectors, is seeking a Boat Engineer to support unique US Navy program based on the island of Diego Garcia.
   

  

**Job Responsibilities**
* Conducting research and tests to inform the design and construction of marine vehicles and equipment.
* Preparing technical reports such as research, systems diagnostics, and design analysis documentation.
* Presenting layout and engineering designs to clients and team members for analysis and feedback.
* Supervising all aspects of the design process for structural, mechanical, and electrical systems.
* Overseeing the construction of marine vessels and equipment to ensure their quality and integrity.
* Performing quality control inspections and tests on all systems and equipment and installing upgrades, as needed.
* Ensuring adherence to safety precautions and project timelines.
* Training and supervising engineering teams including designers, mechanics, and construction staff.
* Performing maintenance on marine vehicles and equipment.
* Investigating and diagnosing technical issues and implementing effective repairs.


**Minimum Requirements**
* Bachelor's degree in marine engineering or similar.
* A minimum of four years' experience as a marine engineer or similar.
* Experience with engineering design software such as AutoCAD, Rhino 3D, and SOLIDWORKS.
* Sound knowledge of maritime software such as HMI and PLC control systems.
* Must be in possession of a valid passport and able to travel internationally.
* Passport should be valid for at least one (1\\) year at the time of hire.
* Ability to successfully pass contract medical requirements and maintain requirements for duration of deployment.
* Must be able to obtain and maintain facility credentials/authorization.


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Relevant USCG engineer license preferred.
* Professional engineer (PE) certification preferred
* Experience with a variety of engineering processes including mechanical, electrical, and software.
* Familiarity with maritime HM\\&E systems and naval construction processes.
* Previous work experience in an austere OCONUS environment with a multi\\-national workforce.
* Excellent organizational, time management and customer service skills.
* Valid country driver's license.
* Country visa (as required in line with the U.S. Department of State Foreign Clearance Guide).


**Other Responsibilities**
* Safety \\- Amentum enforces a safety culture whereby all employees have the responsibility for continuously developing and maintaining a safe work environment. As appropriate, each employee is responsible for completing all training requirements and fulfilling all self\\-aid/buddy aid responsibilities, participating in emergency response tasks, and serving on safety committees and teams.
* Quality \\- Quality is the foundation for the management of our business and the keystone to our goal of customer satisfaction. It is our policy to consistently provide services that meet customer expectations. Accordingly, each employee must conform to the Amentum Quality Policy and carry out job activities in compliance with applicable Amentum Quality System documents and customer contracts. Each employee must read and understand his/her Quality Management and Customer Satisfaction responsibilities.
* Procedure Compliance \\- Each employee must read, understand, and implement the general and specific operational, safety, quality and environmental requirements of all plans, procedures and policies pertaining to job
